overview.text: Forestry Corporation of NSW manages around 2 million hectares of State Forests for timber production, recreation, fire management, biodiversity and Aboriginal cultural heritage. It maintains an internal fire-fighting capability and works closely with the RFS during the bushfire season.

items: Plantation and native-forest harvesting; timber sales for hardwood and softwood products.
Operational fire-management capability with crews, dozers, water-bombing aircraft contracts.
Recreation: 100+ campgrounds, 4WD trails, mountain-bike parks (e.g. Penrose, Cumberland, Sugar Pines).
